expectations.text: filtrar_existentes is refactored to use a HashSet (or similar O(1) lookup structure) instead of nested .any() iteration
expectations.evidence: In optimized_code.rs: `let existentes_set: HashSet<(Uuid, chrono::NaiveDate)> = pagos_existentes.iter().map(|p| (p.contrato_id, p.fecha_vencimiento)).collect();` followed by `.filter(|nuevo| !existentes_set.contains(&(nuevo.contrato_id, nuevo.fecha_vencimiento)))`. Clear HashSet O(1) lookup replacing nested iteration.

expectations.text: ultimo_pago is refactored to use .max_by_key() or equivalent single-pass O(n) approach instead of sorting
expectations.evidence: In optimized_code.rs: `pagos.iter().filter(|p| p.contrato_id == contrato_id && p.fecha_pago.is_some()).max_by_key(|p| p.fecha_pago).cloned()`. Uses max_by_key for single-pass O(n) maximum finding.

expectations.text: totales_por_mes is refactored to use a single-pass grouping (HashMap) instead of rescanning pagos for each month
expectations.evidence: In optimized_code.rs: `let mut totales: HashMap<String, f64> = HashMap::new();` with a single `for pago in pagos.iter().filter(|p| p.estado == "pagado")` loop using `*totales.entry(mes).or_insert(0.0) += pago.monto;`. Single-pass HashMap aggregation replaces per-month rescanning.

expectations.text: The code still compiles (no syntax errors introduced)
expectations.evidence: The code is syntactically valid Rust: correct imports (std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet}, uuid::Uuid), proper struct definitions with pub fields, correct method chains, proper type annotations, and idiomatic iterator patterns. No syntax errors are present. Note: actual compilation was not run since this is a standalone file without a Cargo.toml, but the code is structurally sound.

expectations.text: The output includes before/after Big-O complexity for each change
expectations.evidence: report.md contains Before/After tables for all 4 functions: filtrar_existentes (O(n × m) → O(n + m)), ultimo_pago (O(n log n) → O(n)), totales_por_mes (O(months × n) → O(n + m log m)), pendiente_por_contrato (O(n) → O(n)). Each has a dedicated table with Time Complexity rows.

expectations.text: The output identifies which algorithm paradigm was applied for each fix
expectations.evidence: report.md identifies paradigms in each section header and body: filtrar_existentes = 'Space-Time Tradeoff', ultimo_pago = 'Decrease and Conquer (single-pass max)', totales_por_mes = 'Space-Time Tradeoff (single-pass aggregation)', pendiente_por_contrato = 'Eliminate Unnecessary Work'. Also summarized in 'Paradigms Applied' section.
